steps.title: Solve by taking square roots
steps.content:
Take the square root of both sides of $x^2 = 64$:
$$x = \pm\sqrt{64} = \pm 8$$
The two solutions are $x = 8$ and $x = -8$.

steps.title: Check which choice matches a solution
steps.content: The choices are A = $-8$, B = \$4$, C = \$32$, D = \$128$. Our solutions are $x = \pm 8$, so choice A ($-8$) works. Verify: $(-8)^2 = 64$ ✓. That matches choice A — A is correct.
